originalTitle: South Africa's long-serving former defence minister and former Robben Island prisoner dies
title: A Brave Helper for South Africa Goes to Rest
content:
Hello, my little friends! I have some news to share with you from our big, beautiful continent of Africa, especially from a country called South Africa. Sometimes, special people who have helped make our world a better place go to rest. Today, we are thinking about one such brave man, his name was **Mr. Mosiuoa Lekota**.

Our Hero, Mr. Mosiuoa Lekota

Mr. Lekota was a very, very important person. He was like a superhero who helped bring fairness and kindness to everyone in South Africa. He lived a very long life, filled with courage and helping others. When grown-ups talk about him, they say he was a 'former defence minister' – that's a big grown-up job, like a super-captain who helps keep a country safe and sound. He also spent time on a special, famous island, but we'll talk more about that soon! Mr. Lekota believed that everyone, no matter what they looked like or where they came from, should be treated with love and respect. He wanted all children to have happy lives and grow up in a fair country. That's why he worked so hard, all his life, to make South Africa a wonderful place for every single person.

The Special Island Prison: Robben Island

Many, many years ago, when Mr. Lekota was a younger man, there were some very unfair rules in South Africa. Because he wanted these unfair rules to change, he was put in a very famous, but tough, prison. This prison was on an island far out in the sea, called **Robben Island**. Imagine an island where good people were kept, just because they asked for fairness! **Did you know?** Robben Island is a real island in the ocean, near the city of Cape Town in South Africa. Now, it's a very special museum that teaches people about bravery and fairness! On Robben Island, Mr. Lekota was not alone. He was with another very, very famous hero from South Africa, a man you might have heard of – **Mr. Nelson Mandela**. Mr. Mandela was also in prison for a very long time because he also fought against unfair rules. They were like brave friends, standing up for what was right, even when it was very hard. They spent many years in that prison, but they never, ever gave up on their dream of a fair South Africa. They believed that one day, everyone would live together happily, like a big, colourful family.

What Were the Unfair Rules?

Many years ago, in South Africa, there was something called 'apartheid'. This is a big, grown-up word, but it just means that rules were very, very unfair. Imagine if some children were told they couldn't play with others because of the colour of their skin, or couldn't go to the same school, or even live in the same place. That's how unfair it was! Mr. Lekota and Mr. Mandela and many other brave people said, "No! This is not right! Everyone is special and everyone deserves to be treated fairly." They spoke out and worked very hard to change these rules, so that all children and all people in South Africa could live together, play together, and go to school together. **Did you know?** South Africa is sometimes called the 'Rainbow Nation' because it has so many different kinds of people living together, like the beautiful colours of a rainbow!

After Prison: Helping South Africa Grow

After a very long time, the unfair rules finally changed! Mr. Lekota and Mr. Mandela, and all the other brave people, were set free from prison. And guess what? They didn't stop helping! Mr. Lekota became a very important helper in the government. He was called the 'defence minister'. Imagine if your country was like a big house, and he was one of the people who helped make sure everyone in the house was safe and sound. He worked very hard to build a new, fair South Africa, a place where everyone felt safe and had a chance to be happy. He helped make sure the country's protectors, like the army and police, looked after all people equally. He worked with Mr. Nelson Mandela and many others to make sure that the bad old rules never came back and that South Africa would be a happy and peaceful home for everyone.

Why Mr. Lekota Was a Hero

Mr. Mosiuoa Lekota was a hero because: * He was super brave! He stood up for what was right, even when it was scary. * He was kind. He wanted everyone to be treated with love and respect. * He never gave up. Even in prison, he kept hoping and working for a better future. * He helped make South Africa a country where all children can grow up happy and free. He taught us that it's important to be fair, to be kind, and to always stand up for people who might be treated unfairly.

Let's Remember Him

Even though Mr. Mosiuoa Lekota has gone to rest, his story will always be remembered. He helped build a country where everyone is special, just like you! So, let's remember his bravery and try to be kind and fair to everyone we meet, every single day. That's the best way to honour heroes like Mr. Lekota. **Did you know?** There are 11 official languages in South Africa! This means many people speak different languages, but they all learn to live and play together in peace and friendship!
summary: Mr. Mosiuoa Lekota, a brave South African hero who fought for fairness and was imprisoned on Robben Island with Nelson Mandela, has passed away, leaving behind a legacy of courage and kindness.
whyMatters: Mr. Lekota was very important because he helped make South Africa a fair country for everyone, showing us all to be brave and kind and to stand up for what is right.

originalTitle: Ethiopia experiments with 'smart' police stations that have no officers
title: Super Smart Safety Houses in Ethiopia!
content:
Hello, Super Friends!

Hi everyone! It's your friendly African news reporter here, ready to tell you about something super cool happening in a country called Ethiopia! Do you know where Ethiopia is? It's a beautiful country right here in Africa, full of amazing people and stories. Today, we're talking about something that sounds like it's from a future movie: 'smart' police stations! Can you imagine a police station without any police officers inside? It sounds a bit strange, right? But Ethiopia is trying something very new and very exciting to help keep everyone safe and sound. Let's find out more about these special places!

What Are These 'Smart' Stations?

Imagine you need help, but the police station is far away, or maybe it's late at night. In Ethiopia, they are trying out special 'help houses' – we call them 'smart' police stations. These aren't houses with real people sitting inside all the time. Instead, they are super clever places with big screens and computers! When you go there, you can talk to a real police officer, but they are on a screen, just like a video call with your family or friends! It’s like magic, but it’s real-life technology helping us. These stations are super modern and are there to make it easier for people to get help quickly. Did you know? Ethiopia is one of the oldest countries in Africa and has a rich history, with many ancient traditions and wonderful food!

How Do They Work?

It's very simple and super easy to use! If you need help, you can walk up to one of these special smart stations. Inside, there might be a big screen and a button. You press the button, and *poof*! A friendly police officer appears on the screen, ready to listen to you. You can tell them if you lost something, or if you need to report something important. They can see you and hear you, and they can even help you fill out forms or give you advice. It’s like having a helpful friend always ready to chat and solve problems, even if they are not in the same room as you! These stations are always open, which is really helpful. Whether it's early morning or late at night, the smart station can be there to connect you with help. This means help is closer to many more people, especially in busy cities or places that don't have a big police building nearby. It's all about making safety and help accessible to everyone, everywhere.

Why is Ethiopia Trying This New Idea?

Ethiopia is a country that loves to try new and 'smart' ways to do things. They want to use the best new 'digital technology' to make life better for everyone. Digital technology means using computers, screens, and the internet to help us. Think of your parents' smartphones or tablets – that's digital technology! By using these smart stations, the police can help more people faster. If officers don't have to sit in every little station, they can be out in the community, helping in other ways, or answering calls from many smart stations at once! It's like when you use a computer to do many things at the same time. These smart stations are an 'experiment,' which means they are trying them out to see how well they work and if people like them. If they work really well, maybe more countries in Africa and around the world will try them too! It's a step into the future of helping people and keeping communities safe. Did you know? The internet helps us connect with people all over the world, even if they are very far away! It's how these smart stations connect you to a police officer.

What is 'Digital Technology'?

Digital technology sounds like a big, grown-up word, but it's really simple! It means using smart machines like computers, tablets, and phones to do things. These machines use electricity to think and help us. For example, when you play a game on a tablet, or when your parents talk on a video call, that's digital technology! These smart police stations use digital technology to connect you to an officer through a screen. It makes things faster, easier, and can help many people at the same time. Think of traffic lights, too. They use smart technology to help cars and people move safely. Digital technology is all around us, helping us every day, even when we don't even notice it! It’s making our world a 'smarter' place.

Is it Safe?

Yes, absolutely! These smart stations are made to be very safe. Even though there isn't a police officer sitting inside the station all the time, there are still real police officers working hard, just in a different place. They are ready to come and help if someone needs them in person. The smart stations usually have cameras to keep an eye on things, and they are built to be strong and secure. It's like having an extra pair of eyes and ears, always ready to help you connect with the right person. You can feel safe knowing that help is always just a screen away. Did you know? Many schools now use computers and tablets to help children learn new things and connect with teachers, making learning extra fun!

Who Benefits from These Smart Stations?

Everyone benefits! First, people who live far from big police stations can get help much faster. It's like having a small help center closer to their homes. Second, it helps the police because they can use their officers' time in the best way possible. An officer can talk to someone on screen and also be ready to go out and help in person if needed. Third, it can make people feel safer because they know help is always available, day or night. It shows that Ethiopia is thinking about new and clever ways to take care of its wonderful people. These smart stations are a great example of how technology can be used for good, to make our communities safer and stronger. It's exciting to see new ideas like this helping people right here in Africa!

Looking to the Future

This is just the beginning of many new and exciting things! Ethiopia is showing us how we can use smart ideas and technology to improve our lives. Maybe one day, we will see smart stations that help with other things, like finding a lost pet or learning about fun events in the community. The future is full of possibilities, and it's wonderful to see Africa leading the way with such clever solutions to help everyone. Keep an eye out for more super smart news! Did you know? In some places, smart robots are even being used to help deliver food or clean houses! Technology is changing the world very fast!
summary: Ethiopia is trying out new 'smart' police stations where people can talk to police officers on a screen, making it easier and faster for everyone to get help using cool digital technology.
whyMatters: This is important because it shows how new computers and screens can help keep people safe and make police services easier to reach for everyone in Africa, even in places far from big police offices.
